reviews.review_description:
Good looking game, but every room seems to look basically the same.

Walking speed is dire.

Weapon physics are a bit hit and miss for me. On the one hand it feels a little rubbery, contact doesn't have much feedback, the bow likes to randomly move around where it feels like, which throws your shots off.

Elemental magic attacks are pretty good, but mana runs down fast at this early stage.

Enemies have varied attack moves and ranged abilities while you have no defences, dodges etc to properly counter them. There is a lot of walking backwards.

Odd number health pickups are often very stingy when compared to the amount of damage you can take.

Audio feedback on enemy spawning and movement is quite minimal and leads to a lot of backstabbing.

Inventory interaction is a bit hit and miss, leading to failure to grab weapons when needed.

The blacksmith/upgrade system is kinda weird and confusing. Even just the addition of some labels would help here.

First time running it crashed when loading between the tutorial and lobby area. Had to reboot and it worked. But the lobby was pretty weird and there was a huge frame rate drop. After that I got disconnected and offline game went smoothly.

Overall impression: it looks good, plays mid to OK, but is quite a step below other games like DoE in terms of physics, inventory and weapon interactions, and variety. With tweaks and upgrades and some more variety in the environment it could be an interesting title but there's definitely something missing at this stage. One to keep an eye on.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback!    |    Some of the things you mentioned have been patched out:     |    - The bow issue has been fixed    |    - Servers have been fixed   |   - The weight of the weapons and shields has been reduced, making them easier to swing.    |   - The handguards of the weapons now deal damage -> No more unregistered hits.   |   - Parrying attacks is now significantly easier with swords and shields.   |   - Slightly reduced the swing speed threshold required to deal damage.

   |   Let me know what you think of these changes.

reviews.review_description:
Good game for a good price, nothing gets better than this. 

But there is a few bugs though and it doesnt feel “polished” but its early access anyways and right now is the best time to get the game since the price will only be going up from here.

PS. Use this promo-code for 15% off:
                 15Launch-B8633E

reviews.review_title: A NEW KID ON THE CRAWLER BLOCK
reviews.review_description:
Now if you love a dungeon crawler, this really fits the  bill nicely, the Quest 3 graphics are such a lovely bonus, everything looks clean and sharp, the tutorial is very intuitive and really gets you ready for what lies ahead.
The hub area is nice, I’m a solo player, and jumped in for some quick action, hit detection is spot on & feels really weighty when you wave you sword, for an early access game this already feels quite polished, so I can’t wait for what the dev has planned.
Very impressive so far. Great promising start.

*Early Access Review*

Impressive!
The graphics are quite nice and atmospheric.
Sound effects are good.
Music is medieval and atmospheric

Game play allows you to play solo or multiplayer.. I played solo. You can have the option to allow others to join you during your game.

You start with a simple easy tutorial done in the way of message boards.

Then you start the game with a bow on your back, sword and shield on your waist, and a pick axe and potions on your bag. Also a spell for each hand.

You go around the dungeons which are randomly generated each time, and are quite big… killing the enemies, and mining the crystals which you use to upgrade equipment.

There is bosses to kill that give you a chest of looooooot!!!!

But also as you are hacking your way through the dungeon, enemies also drop random loot that seems to be in the form of item blueprints which you craft back at your base/hub.

When you die, you are sent back to your base/hub where you can craft various items like helms, armour, shields, weapons,.. and equip the various items and spells too ready for your next adventure.

When you craft a new item, you have to grab the hammer at the blacksmith and hammer away on the anvil to craft it.
Items also have various skins that you can change to which cost coins.
Items give various stat bonuses too.

I think this is a great game and would be very fun when played with friends.

I do think your hands and maybe items are a little too big though.. but maybe the dev will change this as the game develops and comes out of early access.

This is a good game that follows the Dungeons of Eternity formula.  The combat feels decent with weighted weapons that have inertia.  I like the way magic is implemented an hope this system expands into more elaborate gesture based casting.  The graphics are stylistic giving me the impression that I am in a artist's rendering on the cover of a 1980's Dungeons and Dragons module.  Really a fantastic job.  But.....

The main thing I came here to say is that the breakable pottery and barrels is OUT OF HAND.  I know this has become a trope and nearly all RPG games are doing it these days, but man...  It's really a chore to have to go through and break dozens and dozens of pots EVERY game often with like 6 - 10 pots in EACH ROOM.  So you enter a room, have a fun combat encounter then you have to spend nearly as much time in the room as you did fighting the monsters going around and breaking this pot, then this pot, then go over here and break this pot, oh there's a barrel over there.  More pots.  Break the table, another table, oh three more pots... (sigh).  It's immersion breaking and begins to feel like labor.  I REALLY wish games would start to move away from this.  It's actually beginning to make me not want to play.  Again, not the only game that does it.

developer_response.body:
You do not have to break anything, it is completely optional to do when you happen to walk past those props.
You only get a little bit of money and small potions which you can also buy from the shop.

reviews.review_description: Love they way u interact with evrything smashing barrels shelves and pot tables chairs lol hoovering up money feels good too. The only nitpick I have is maybe add a map and difficulty tiers in each dungeons. Other than that great game. Also making a game with 1 person at the helm takes a lot of time and resources. I urge anybody reading this comment to buy this fantastic game for the price of few coffees u can't go wrong. Looking forward to making dungeon friends along the way 👍

Was lucky to win a key for this in a Reddit giveaway.
First impressions are of strong potential. The enemies look amazing. It is quite unforgiving - first room first game was about 6 different mobs. Not sure I have the knack of the magic yet. Sword definitely useless when mobs run into you, have to back off to use it and needs decent room to swing. Also looks like stabbing doesn't work, only swinging. No issues with the bow that others have had but the mobs are smart enough to move out the way. 
The blacksmith/upgrade area is down the steps to the right of the dungeon launcher
Suggestions:
Please add a mini map! Easy to get turned around during battle or when retreating and ive spent more than a few minutes going in the wrong direction or wandering around trying to trigger new enemies.
One to come back to for sure

I've been looking forward to this for a while, I hope the full release brings a great game. 

Good bones to start. 
Art style
Blood splatter. 
Magic casting is fun. 
Get loot at a good rate
Co-op, and offline single player available. 
Hopes and needed improvement: 
-Should be able to attack enemies with shield. 
-Thrusting mechanics for sword needed. Should be able to stab
-Sprint is not great (I recommend that speed actually be default and add a dash)
-Just add a dash ;) 
-You should be able to toggle grip. 
-You should be able to toggle sprit rather than holding it down. 
-We need more dungeons and maps 
-The fog isn't a bad look but I wouldn't mind a TOUCH more visual draw distance. 
-Would like a jump 
-Would like coins to auto collect when close.
-Your own characters hands look oversized and clunky 
-Would be cool to see weapons stained with blood after a recent fight. 


Hope this helps, love this games style and action. 
Me and two friends got it. High hopes!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback. You can attack with a shield to knock enemies down. Toggle sprinting and toggle gripping have been added to the game now.

reviews.review_description: This game is genuinely one of the best dungeon crawlers i’ve ever played. It even has magic that you can fire from your hands which is epic and so much stuff to craft, find, unlock sorta, and upgrade. The developer absolutely blew my expectations! Yall should get this game cause it’s amazing and it has loads more content to come.

reviews.review_title: Overall Good Game, one big thing I hate.
reviews.review_description:
[Update from my last review about not being able to refund the game, 3 stars -> 4 stars]

This game graphically looks meh in most ways, but oh my god the blood and slashing is easily some of the best ive ever seen, vr or not. Its so satisfying and feels absolutely amazing.

I havent gotten to play online much since the servers are being worked on (or something similar, I forgot the exact reason), so I cant quite give a full accurate review on the feel / content of the game.

Anyways, onto the thing i hate, how large the player model is. Im still not used to it and it feels like im trying to control a mech everywhere I walk around. Im very used to other games either having smaller playermodels or adapting well to larger player models (ex; underdogs), but this game feels off with it for some reason.

developer_response.body:
Hey, (response to the updated review)
Btw the servers have been working really well for many days now.

reviews.review_title: Best dungeon crawler VR game ever
reviews.review_description: I’ve never played such a good dungeon crawler VR game in my life this game is literally just like iron strike but 10 times better there’s blood, you could upgrade your weapons, and you could upgrade your armor.￼ if you’re looking for a dungeon crawler game ￼￼where it has all of the things I just said plus good graphics. This game is for you. ￼

for a 1 dev army, this game is very promising. Its tutorial is a little lacking, but you get the hang of it fast, and it's really good fun solo. I can't wait for multi-player to begin working again. Its combat system is a little tricky, but it's not very difficult. the magic is super strong (which I like), and from what I can tell, so are the melee tanks and rangers, the archery is very finicky and a little hard to aim, but still fun.

 I highly recommend at least trying the game out, and if you don't like it, then just get a refund

The combat with melee weapons is nice and weighted, the details are pretty good for example the shield breaks as you block each attack. The weapon crafting is a nice. The magic is pretty cool and powerful with mana limiting you. This game is amazing for the price of it. I can't wait to get more content from this game.

I honestly did not think that I was going to like this game as much as I did! First thing that I noticed was the enemies have a Warcraftian art style which I love! For an early access title it  looks and plays really smoothly. The weapons feel good and impactful and being able to shoot electricity and fire out of your hands when you get surrounded is pretty cool. I'm a huge fan of dungeon crawlers and this scratches that itch. I totally believe that this game will be able to hang with  the big boys Dungeons of Eternity And Legendary tales.  Cant wait to see what the developer does with this game!

reviews.review_title: Early Access Review Really Good Game
reviews.review_description: Having a blast so far. If ya like Dungeon Crawlers, Physics base combat with rpg elements I’d say this game is for you.  Game is early access, and made by one Dev who is constantly updating the game.  Jump in early while game is cheap and enjoy the ride

reviews.review_title: Good SO Far - Some Upgrades Referenced
reviews.review_description:
I'd love to see combat closer to Legendary Tales, also the inventory system as well. I think the unlocking is cool. The powers are a nice touch. It seems like it's got its own art style. I wish tho that it somehow affects my bow as I turn it or move. THEY NEED A DASH AND RUN OPTION! ALSO LARGER STORAGE. Also WHEN I BLOCK ENEMIES AT CLOSE RANGE, THEY SOMETIMES WALK THROUGH ME AND I STILL TAKE DAMAGE. ID LIKE TO SEE MORE PLAYER UPGRADES, MAYBE A SKILL TREE, SCRAPPING UNLOCKED ARMOR PIECES FOR JEWELS OR POTIONS OR SOME SORT OF VALUE. 

I FEEL LIKE THERE SHOULD ALSO BE AN ACCELERATION MODE LIKE ONCE YOU LAND A COUPLE BLOWS YOU CAN HIT R2 AND IT UNLEASHES LIKE A SUPER BLOW/FORCE! 

GOOD SO FAR THO! IM PLAYING!

It's not a bad game it just needs some work which is fair because it's an early access. Let's start with the servers are down and you can't play with anybody that's okay you can still play it in single-player mode.
Let's talk about the graphics. The graphics are actually pretty darn good on Standalone Hardware. Moving on to player speed physics and other things. The physics in the handling of weapons isn't bad but it needs some work. Enemies that are up close won't receive any damage from your sword because your sword will just slide across them like a wet noodle. But the biggest offender for me is the bow. Whenever you draw the bow over your shoulder sometimes it will come out with you grabbing the string which makes it unshootable. Then when you try to shoot the bow your drawing hand or you're knocking hand rather, we'll get bent in a funny position and shoot the arrow to the ground or to the air like it's not getting tracked. When you're holding the bow the bow itself will interlock with the body physics of your character and make it impossible to get good shots and aim. All of this together brings an experience that really is lacking in the range fighting Department. Arrows doing everything but what you want them to. I've already upgraded the bow to see if it was an upgrade issue and it's not it's the baked in physics. I love Dungeon Crawlers and I think with a few good patches this game could be really fun to play with other people.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback!     |    - The bow has been fixed and it should not have problems like that anymore.     |   - The server are up and running without issues    |   - The weapon physics and hit registration has been improved

reviews.review_description: Shout out to the solo dev, this game is fun and has a lot of potential to grow into something special. At the moment, it’s a bit bare, but the foundation feels good!

Very good game pretty solid i have enjoyed the combat and the looting system but i would like to see some more enemy types
I really recommend playing the game
So far so good!! Really excited for this game and having a lot of fun!
It's a pain in the a$$ to play not smooth wait for full release then try but it's not worth the headache now
Says no offline or online play.

developer_response.body:
Hello,
What do you mean? Online and offline mode are both available to play.
Join the discord if you are having issues with the game https://discord.gg/GC4CqRebvF

reviews.review_description: Game is great!

reviews.review_title: I 'Delved' right in for an early review.
reviews.review_description:
This game is fast, enemies get strong really quick, and thankfully there is a good tutorial to walk you through your skills before you start.
Graphically its good, vibrant with great sounds, and I can see myself coming back to this, lots to explore lots to break up to finding hidden treasures and potions.
I played as single player as I do not enjoy co-op or playing with ‘randoms’, also there are lots of adjustments in the settings and I look forward to tweaking it a bit more to suit my style of play.
My only concern for me is I did not feel the weight of my blade or my pickaxe and when smashing trunks and urns, I had no feel to it, also my shooting of arrows was very hit and miss but that may be user error and lack of practice.  That said it's still pretty good and as it is still early release and pretty well polished I will give it the full 5 stars and hope the devs tweak the small issues I found.
I was originally not going to buy it due to the very low review count,however I bit the bullet and bought it. For 13$ it’s definitely a good game and you should buy it if you have the money and time

For the 1st day of an Early Access game, it's looking pretty solid in my opinion. Mechanics seem to feel very pretty well to me. Enemy types seem pretty cool. May not have varied amount of levels at the moment but seems to have a decent loot system and even a spell system. It has a glitch or 2, also some server issue but that's to be expected of an Early Access game in the first hour of its launch. I could see this game growing and becoming very good with the mechanics that it offers and loot systems. I'm sure they'll be adding more and cleaning up the game with time.  Seems to be something I will enjoy though and I'm going to continue to play this one.

reviews.review_title: A rough take on Dungeons of Eternity
reviews.review_description:
This is a rough attempt at a Dungeons of Eternity style game that's really not enjoyable to me.

The accelerating smooth locomotion feels odd and a bit uncomfortable. Instead of walking right away you start slow and accelerate up to speed over a brief period of time.  It feels like you're accelerating by slowly moving the stick forward, even when you're not.  There doesn't seem to be a jump ability either.

The combat feels sluggish and it doesn't seem to matter where you strike as far as damage goes. In Dungeons of Eternity it's satisfying to pop the head off a skeleton in one clean shot with the bow. In this game the enemies just collect the arrows in their skulls and keep coming at you.  Casting spells is very difficult. When they successfully fly away from your hand it's a rare stroke of luck.

The upgrade area is presented with no explanation and looks extremely early in its development.

The art direction is unusual, but I really like it. Unfortunately it's the only part of the game I do like.

The full release is due this summer but because it's currently so far away from what I'd want in a Dungeons of Eternity style game I'm going request a refund.
After initial server problem resoved and able to play on single player a bit ( multiplayer not yet running), the game is fun so far although sword wielding is a sluggish. Hopefully this will improve.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback!       |       - The server are up and running without issues now      |      - The weight of the weapons and shields has been reduced, making them easier to swing.      |      - The handguards of the weapons now deal damage -> No more unregistered hits.      |      - Slightly reduced the swing speed threshold required to deal damage.      |      - Parrying attacks is now significantly easier with swords and shields.      |      Let me know if these fixed your issue with the game

reviews.review_description: It’s such a good game especially for early access! Has huge potential once more content is added.
